levels.chooseLevel: Which level do you want to train at?

1.title: Hammer Curls
1.description: Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing your body, and curl both weights up without bending your wrists. This targets the biceps and forearms.

2.title: Concentration Curls
2.description: Sit on a bench, rest your elbow on the inside of your thigh, and slowly curl the dumbbell toward your shoulder. This deeply strengthens the biceps.

3.title: Incline Bicep Curls
3.description: Lie back on an incline bench and curl the dumbbells up, keeping elbows behind your body. This increases stretch and tension on the biceps.

4.title: Reverse Curls
4.description: Hold the dumbbells with palms facing down and curl upward. This shifts the emphasis to the forearm muscles.

5.title: Alternating Bicep Curls
5.description: Curl one dumbbell at a time while keeping the other arm straight, giving each arm a full range of motion.

6.title: Wide Hand Push Up
6.description: Place your hands wider than shoulder-width and lower your chest toward the ground, then push back up. This emphasizes the outer chest muscles.

7.title: Barbell Bench Press
7.description: Lower the barbell to the middle of your chest and push it back up over your shoulders. A key exercise for chest, shoulders, and triceps.

8.title: Bodyweight Squat
8.description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and lower your hips back and down as if sitting in a chair, then rise back up. Builds foundational leg strength.

9.title: Barbell Back Squat
9.description: Rest the barbell on your upper back and squat down until your thighs are parallel to the ground. A heavy exercise for overall leg strength.

10.title: Standing Dumbbell Shoulder Press
10.description: Push the dumbbells up from shoulder level until your arms are fully extended. Builds shoulder strength and stability.

11.title: Plank
11.description: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels, supported on your forearms and toes. Strengthens the entire core.

12.title: Kettlebell Russian Twist
12.description: Sit with knees bent and lean back slightly, rotating the kettlebell from side to side. Targets the obliques and rotational core strength.

13.title: Superman Back Extension
13.description: Lie face down and lift your arms, chest, and legs off the ground at the same time. Strengthens the lower back and improves posture.

14.title: Bench Tricep Dips
14.description: Support your body on a bench with hands behind you and lower your hips toward the ground, then push back up. This isolates the triceps.

15.title: Overhead Triceps Extension
15.description: Hold a dumbbell with both hands behind your head and extend your arms upward. Stretches and strengthens the triceps.

16.title: Goblet Squat
16.description: Hold a dumbbell close to your chest and squat down between your knees. Builds leg strength while keeping the body upright.

17.title: Gym Ball Hamstring Curl
17.description: Lie on your back with heels on an exercise ball, lift your hips, and pull the ball toward you. Targets the hamstrings and glutes.

18.title: Jump Rope Cardio Burst
18.description: Jump continuously with a steady rhythm on the rope. A full-body cardio exercise that also improves coordination.

19.title: Weight Plate Front Raise
19.description: Hold a weight plate with both hands and raise it straight in front of you to shoulder level. Builds the front shoulder muscles.

20.title: Push Up to Plank
20.description: Perform a push up, then hold briefly in a plank position before lowering again. Combines upper body strength with core stability.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.s1Body: We collect information you provide such as name, email, age, height, and weight to personalize your fitness experience. We also collect usage data to improve app performance.
privacyPolicy.s2Body: Your information is used to create personalized workout and diet plans, track your progress, and send the reminders you set.
privacyPolicy.s3Body: Your data is stored securely on your device, and where applicable, on our servers with industry-standard encryption.
privacyPolicy.s4Body: We do not sell your personal information. Limited data may be shared with service providers only to run core app features.
privacyPolicy.s5Body: You can access, update, or delete your account information at any time from the settings menu.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.s1Body: By creating an account and using Doc-rob-fitness, you agree to abide by these Terms & Conditions.
terms.s2Body: Doc-rob-fitness provides fitness tracking, workout plans, and diet guidance for personal use only. You agree not to misuse the app or its content.
terms.s3Body: Doc-rob-fitness is not a substitute for professional medical advice. Consult a physician before starting any new exercise or diet program.
terms.s4Body: Premium features require an active subscription. Subscriptions renew automatically unless cancelled before the renewal date.
terms.s5Body: Doc-rob-fitness is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We are not liable for any injury or damage resulting from use of the app.
